repo_prior_art.cite: pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1 is the closest in-repo concordance-band ancestor, but it targets strict benefit under Spearman transport and was downgraded on no-gap/selector issues; this GPN point-evaluation Kendall envelope is narrower and structurally different.

published_prior_art.cite: ChaogeHanLiuWu2026 gives continuous-outcome GPN Frechet/monotonicity and copula sensitivity; NelsenQuesadaMolinaRodriguezLallenaUbedaFlores2001/NelsenUbedaFlores2004 give noncausal pointwise Kendall copula bounds; no source composes these into a causal sharp Kendall-band GPN theorem. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01883, https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.06915.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal states the Gaussian-copula sensitivity path as a subfamily of a Kendall-band GPN sharp set.

published_prior_art.cite: ChaogeHanLiuWu2026 uses copula-model sensitivity for continuous GPN, so the subfamily-containment comparison is mostly bookkeeping once Theorem 1 is available. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01883.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo entry states this exact no-Kendall-information reduction for the population GPN endpoint formula.

published_prior_art.cite: The Frechet-Hoeffding no-dependence benchmark is already the standard continuous-GPN bound in ChaogeHanLiuWu2026, modulo the estimand-weighting issue flagged below. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01883.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1 and pid_bound_intersection_inference already isolate concordance-bound endpoint inference as the nearby unresolved repo theme, but not this Kendall point-evaluation GPN endpoint EIF.

published_prior_art.cite: HeLi2026 gives fixed-parametric-copula EIFs and one-step estimators; DornGuoKallus2022 gives orthogonal sharp sensitivity-bound estimation for ATE sensitivity, not family-free Kendall GPN endpoints. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.11415, https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.11449.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1 left active-switch concordance endpoint inference open; this narrows the target to tau-indexed Kendall GPN curves.

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 supplies generic intersection-bound inference and HeLi2026 supplies pointwise fixed-copula bands; neither gives uniform inference for family-free Kendall-constrained cross-world endpoint curves with Nelsen active-piece switches. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/0907.3503, https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.11415.

novelty_flags.label: lem:frechet-reduction / related_work comparison to ChaogeHanLiuWu2026
novelty_flags.one_line: ChaogeHanLiuWu2026 defines GPN as conditioning on the observed treated event Z=1,Y>=c1, while this core retargets theta to P(Y(0)<c0 | Y(1)>=c1); the Frechet reduction is exact only after adding constant randomization/e-weighting or downgrading the comparison to an analogous population-target benchmark.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / continuous PoC survey
novelty_flags.one_line: KawakamiKurokiTian2024, Probabilities of Causation for Continuous and Vector Variables (https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.20487), is a plausible continuous-PoC prior omitted from the survey, even though it is monotonicity/identification rather than Kendall-band partial ID.

soundness_flags.label: thm:regular-endpoint-eif / ass:nuisance-rate-m1e / ass:nuisance-rate-m0e
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated DML product-rate assumptions with e are not enough for the nonlinear b_plus/b_minus endpoint maps: with e known the products can be zero while second-order Taylor remainders in hat_m0 and hat_m1 still block root-n CLTs unless squared/cross-nuisance rates or an equivalent smooth-functional remainder condition is added.

soundness_flags.label: prop:gaussian-subfamily consumer text
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposition says the Gaussian sensitivity set is a subset of the Kendall-band sharp set, so calling the Gaussian curve 'conservative' is backwards under the family-free Kendall-band target; it is narrower and can be anti-conservative for that sensitivity class.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:conditional-tau-band
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The P_X-a.e. constant conditional Kendall-tau band is accepted as a novel sensitivity regime, not a derived restriction from observed data.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The sharpness and inference claims are conditional on the stated conditional Kendall-band restriction.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:regular-piece-lower / ass:regular-piece-upper
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Scalar EIF theory is intentionally restricted to locally smooth single-active-piece regimes away from Nelsen envelope kinks.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The root-n scalar endpoint claim is conditional on active-piece uniqueness; active-piece switching is only covered by the separate uniform-inference conjecture.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:uniform-tau-curve-inference
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Uniform tau-curve inference at active-piece switches is acknowledged as open conjectural content.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The curve-band result is accepted only as a conditional solve target, not as an assumed input to the scalar identification theorem.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Decide whether the comparator target is ChaogeHanLiuWu2026's treated-event GPN or the proposal's population counterfactual GPN; either add e(X)-weighted endpoints for the former, assume constant randomization, or rewrite the Frechet lemma as an analogous population-target sanity check.

Add a nonlinear-functional remainder condition for Theorem 2, such as ||hat_m0-m0||_2^2 + ||hat_m1-m1||_2^2 + ||hat_m0-m0||_2||hat_m1-m1||_2 = o_P(n^{-1/2}) on each smooth active piece, or derive a weaker condition tailored to b_plus/b_minus.
Add KawakamiKurokiTian2024 to related work as a continuous/vector PoC precursor and explain that its monotonicity identification theorem is orthogonal to the Kendall-band partial-ID target.

Give a citation location anchor for the exact Nelsen fixed-Kendall pointwise-bound formulas used to define b_plus and b_minus, including how the singleton-tau formula is aggregated over a tau interval.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: fix the Chaoge estimand comparison and strengthen the scalar EIF nuisance-rate conditions before advancing to Stage 0.
